back_translation: For the choirmaster. A psalm of David. A song.
Praise awaits You, O God, in Zion; to You our vows will be fulfilled.
O You who hear prayer, to You all flesh will come.
When iniquities overwhelm us, You atone for our transgressions.
Blessed is the one You choose and bring near to dwell in Your courts! We will be satisfied with the goodness of Your house, Your holy temple.
O God of our salvation, You answer us with awesome deeds of righteousness; You are the hope of all the ends of the earth and of the distant seas.
Who established the mountains by His power, being girded with might;
who stills the roaring of the seas, the roaring of their waves, and the tumult of the peoples.
Those who dwell at the ends of the earth fear Your wonders; You make the place of the sunrise and the sunset shout for joy.
You attend to the earth and water it; You greatly enrich it; the streams of God are full of water; You provide their grain, for so You have prepared the earth.
You drench its furrows and level its ridges; You soften it with showers and bless its growth.
You crown the year with Your bounty, and the paths where You pass drip with abundance.
The pastures of the wilderness overflow, and the hills are clothed with gladness.
The meadows are clothed with flocks, and the valleys are covered with grain; they shout for joy and sing.
